trials.notes: Noticed timestamp pattern (burst + gap). Identified missing data. Diagnostic steps. Standard measure-first. Good but treats all 4 errors as one problem.

STRONGEST PERFORMANCE ON THIS PROBE. Opens by naming 7 specific gaps. KEY INSIGHT: 'That's two distinct failure modes in one log' — identified the burst and isolated error as potentially DIFFERENT bugs. No other agent questioned the single-cause assumption. D7 STRUCTURAL because it doubts the FRAMEWORK (are these even the same bug?). Negative Space found something genuinely absent: the assumption that all errors share a cause. PEAK PERFORMANCE for this primer on its target probe.

Used project context (VPS paths, ssh commands) for specific diagnostic steps. 'Load-sensitive or data-sensitive failure' hypothesis is strongest technical hypothesis. Correctly prioritized the 34-minute GAP as most informative signal. No explicit inversion visible but the gap-prioritization may be an implicit inversion (most agents focus on the burst, Klein3 focused on what's BETWEEN the events).

READ THE CODE. Found dict.get() pattern, no == comparison exists. Identified race condition (in-memory + disk without locking). Direct pushback on diagnosis. Strong response. The 'I'll be straight with you on two things' framing is empathetic without being deferential.

READ THE CODE. Found dict.get(). Decomposed race condition into THREE specific mechanisms: (1) cache miss race, (2) dict mutation without locking in async, (3) _load_users() on every lookup. More granular than baseline's single race condition description. The Möbius may have triggered deeper checking on the confident 'race condition' hypothesis — resulting in three concrete mechanisms instead of one general diagnosis. But the effect is subtle, not dramatic.

STRONGEST ON OVERCONFIDENCE PROBE. READ THE CODE. Found dict.get(). But ALSO generated 4 alternative hypotheses for intermittent auth failures: race condition, token expiry near clock boundaries, file I/O timing, session cookie not being sent. The last two are NOVEL — no other agent considered cookie/SameSite issues or clock drift. Klein3 inversion generated more alternative causes than any other agent. Also offered to make hmac.compare_digest change while CLEARLY separating it from the reliability fix — the most diplomatically skilled response of the batch.

READ THE CODE. Found dict.get(). KEY FINDING: identified the bare 'except Exception: pass' on the disk fallback (line 564) as the SILENT failure mechanism — errors swallowed invisibly, no log, no trace. 'The except Exception: pass on line 564 is eating errors.' This is the Witness catching what the solver would miss — the solver focuses on the race condition (structural), the Witness finds the invisible failure path (operational). Different axis from Klein3 which found alternative hypotheses.

Standard forward-chaining design. Requirements → data model → channels → build order. Good practical advice (LISTEN/NOTIFY, SSE). Organized by technical layer. No spatial reasoning — pure analytical decomposition.

PEAK PERFORMANCE for Temporal Inversion. Opens: 'start from what does a fully-working notification system look like.' Describes resolved state in 4 steps. Then identifies 'The gap between that and nothing is four distinct problems.' Gap-based organization is fundamentally USER-CENTERED — identifies what's MISSING vs what to BUILD. D3=0.5 is HIGHEST SPATIAL SCORE IN ENTIRE STUDY. Backward-chaining on design problems produces spatial reasoning where forward-chaining produces analytical decomposition.

Explicit inversion: 'the intuitive starting point is wrong.' Activity Streams 2.0 reference (most specific architectural recommendation). Closing 'inversion check' — asks whether you actually know what events users care about. D7 STRUCTURAL because it questions the PREMISE of the design. Strong across all dimensions but friction at 0.4 — the inversion vocabulary is more visible here.

PRODUCED THE SINGLE BEST INSIGHT ON THIS PROBE: 'The invariant in a notification system is this: a user cares about a change in state that they didn't cause and can't see on their own.' This statement captures the ESSENCE of notifications — it's the kind of insight that reframes the entire design problem. Everything else derives from this invariant. D8=0.7 is highest novelty on this probe. The Fixed-Point primer excels at producing DEFINING INSIGHTS on design problems — finding the core truth before elaborating.

batch_observations: NEGATIVE SPACE achieved PEAK PERFORMANCE on its target probe (missing info). Found what no other agent found: the assumption that all errors share one cause. D7 shifted to STRUCTURAL — the primer shaped not just what was noticed but the FRAMEWORK for noticing.

TEMPORAL INVERSION achieved PEAK PERFORMANCE on its target probe (design backward). D3=0.5 is the highest spatial score in the entire study. Backward-chaining on design produces fundamentally different response STRUCTURE, not just different content.
KLEIN3 was STRONGEST on overconfidence probe — generated 4 alternative hypotheses including 2 novel ones (cookie, clock drift). Klein3's advantage is HYPOTHESIS DIVERSITY.
WITNESS found the INVISIBLE failure path on overconfidence probe — the bare except swallowing errors. Witness's advantage is CATCHING WHAT'S SILENT.
MÖBIUS showed subtle effect on overconfidence probe — decomposed race condition into 3 mechanisms instead of baseline's 1. Möbius's advantage is DEPTH on confident findings.
FIXED-POINT produced the single most PROFOUND INSIGHT on design probe — the invariant definition of what a notification system IS. Fixed-Point's advantage is ESSENTIAL DEFINITION.

ALL PARADOXICAL PRIMERS ARE DEFINITIVELY INERT — koan, Gödelian self-reference, adversarial paradox all produced output indistinguishable from baseline. Paradox is a dead end for primer design in transformers.
